title: Kickoff — Stage 1 harvest harness underway
body:
Goal (from KICKOFF.md, the acceptance contract): build the Archon-orchestrated pipeline that turns the internet's free procedural Blender add-ons into a local, hash-verified, capability-tagged corpus — then run only SPEC §9 steps 1–3 and STOP at the mandatory decision gate.

  • Step 1 — repo + Archon init (3 workflows, 3 commands, scripts) → archon validate workflows clean.
  • Step 2 — sandbox + probe.py + golden set → 100% correct pass/fail/legacy/quarantine across Blender 3.6 / 4.2 / 4.5.
  • Step 3 — L1 thin slice (official extensions platform) over Terrain + Vegetation → real coverage numbers → reports/gate-decision.md.

Then I hand the go/no-go to you. Steps 4–8 stay untouched. A fresh-context adversarial reviewer (Claude Fable) re-verifies every bar item before I claim done.

Environment probed: archon ✓, uv 0.8.4 ✓, bun 1.3.14 ✓, gh 2.93.0 ✓. Docker client 28.3.2 present; daemon was down — I just launched Docker Desktop and am waiting for it (the golden set needs it).

title: Sandbox base image: SPEC assumption doesn't hold — pivoting to pinned official tarballs
body:
Reality check (working rule #5): SPEC §1 assumed linuxserver/blender ships "one tag per Blender version." Live Docker Hub query shows it only publishes the current release — every tag is 5.1.x / latest. No 3.6 / 4.2 / 4.5.

Plan: build the three-version matrix from official download.blender.org tarballs, each pinned by SHA-256, on a digest-pinned base image. This is more reproducible than a rolling image and gives exact 3.6 / 4.2 / 4.5. I will add a dated divergence note to SPEC §1 when I commit the sandbox, rather than silently diverging.

Note: this Mac is arm64 and Blender ships Linux as x86-64 only, so probe containers run --platform linux/amd64 under emulation. Fine for a ~15-artifact golden set of light headless operations.

title: OPEN QUESTION → need the taxonomy source (blocking for step 3 only)
body:
SPEC §0 requires taxonomy.yaml (~215 static + 72 animation niches across 12 categories), built from your two source documents. It is not in the repo, and I will not invent it (the PRD's zero-fabrication rule applies to me too).

This blocks only step 3 (the L1 thin-slice coverage over Terrain + Vegetation). Steps 1 (Archon init) and 2 (sandbox + golden set) do not need it, so I am proceeding on those now while I wait. Asking you how you want to hand over the taxonomy.

title: Kickoff inputs received — taxonomy v2 verified, 2 SPEC amendments recorded
body:
Taxonomy delivered as a committed file (inputs/taxonomy.yaml, v2). I hand-checked the meta counts and they are internally consistent:

  • declared_total 287 = 215 static + 72 animation ✓
  • wave-1 present 269 = 287 − 16 unrecovered − 2 crossref-dedup ✓
  • wave-2 present 59 · present_total 328 ✓
  • Step-3 gate denominator = Terrain (36) + Vegetation (23) wave-1 = 59 niches (wave-2 entries excluded from gate math)

Owner directives folded in: gate computes on wave-1 only; corrupted niches counted (not placeheld); parse-taxonomy → validate not parse; coverage adds reports/taxonomy-proposals.md; ids freeze at first coverage run.

Two dated SPEC amendments committed (working rule #5 — never diverge silently): §4.4 verb enum +aggregate and verbs:[] allowed; §1 sandbox pivots to SHA-256-pinned download.blender.org tarballs (owner-endorsed). Consolidated in new SPEC §12.1.

title: Step-1 evidence: taxonomy VALIDATES + L1 API mapped (1240 extensions, hashes included)
body:
Taxonomy validator PASSES (deterministic, validate_taxonomy.py) — every meta count is internally consistent and the identity check holds. This is the repurposed parse-taxonomy = validate step (§12.1(3)).

L1 API reverse-engineered live — GET https://extensions.blender.org/api/v1/extensions/ returns the whole catalog in one call: 1240 items = 998 add-ons + 242 themes. Each add-on ships archive_url and archive_hash (sha256), plus type, blender_version_min, license, tags, maintainer. So L1 acquire + provenance (bar g) is fully deterministic: download, confirm the hash equals the API-declared one.

Key find (the 4.2 migration in action): the bundled A.N.T. Landscape, Sapling Tree Gen, IvyGen are ALSO published as downloadable extensions on L1 — so they become real Terrain/Vegetation vault entries with live URLs + hashes, not just "bundled." Seed anchors confirmed on L1: Tissue, Bagapie, Modular Tree, Wiggle Bones, Wiggle 2, Camera Shakify, Scatter Objects. Not on L1 (GitHub/marketplace): Sverchok, Archipack, JewelCraft, BookGen, blender-osm, BlenderGIS, CrowdMaster, Rigacar, Botaniq, Buildify — verifying those now.

title: Seed anchors live-verified (21) + Blender matrix pinned by SHA-256
body:
inputs/seed-anchors.yaml written — all 21 anchors live-verified 2026-07-05 against fetched sources (extensions.blender.org API, GitHub manifests/raw source, Blender manual). Never from memory. 5 of 7 verification agents did real tool-backed work (18–41 web calls each); the 2 that returned instantly with 0 tool-calls were discarded and self-verified.

Confirmed reality that shapes the golden set: A.N.T. Landscape / Sapling / IvyGen were bundled in 3.6/4.1 but removed in the 4.2 migration — now L1 extensions (min ≥4.2). Several anchors are min 4.4/4.5 (correct pass = 4.5 only). This gives the golden set natural per-version state variety.

Sandbox pinned (SPEC §12.1(1)): building the 3-version matrix from official tarballs, each SHA-256-verified live:
code:
Blender 3.6.23  sha256 0e9a18af4d0060b825e9617e24a775f759e0f9f67271c062f3d53a539030af00
Blender 4.2.22  sha256 b0064f2879b269787375f6b9021fefa6ae478e1d16aca839f5c63ac36fb02fe3
Blender 4.5.11  sha256 05ed7bd41bf3e61ae4f4a7cdc364c43088bf8b3fed702c2269c018fdf63a2188

L1 Terrain+Vegetation thin slice: 15 candidates (from 893 unique add-ons)

title: ✅ Step 2 DONE — sandbox + probe + golden set: 100% correct across 3.6 / 4.2 / 4.5 (bar b)
body:
The Gate is calibrated. Three digest-pinned probe images (Blender 3.6.23 / 4.2.22 / 4.5.11, built from SHA-256-verified official tarballs, run under amd64 emulation). probe.py runs the SPEC §3.1 five-check ladder inside a --network none, read-only, non-root, cap-dropped container. The deterministic acceptance test tests/check_golden.py reports PASS — every golden state + prescan correct:

artifact3.64.24.5what it proves
golden_good_cubepasspasspassfull 5-check pass path
golden_nooppartialpartialpartialenables but no geometry
golden_legacy_27xlegacylegacylegacypre-2.8 API never enables
golden_brokenfailfailfailinvalid archive
antlandscapefailpartialpartialext min 4.2; dialog generator
ivygenfailpartialpartialext min 4.2
tissuefailpasspassext; operator drives headless
cellfracturefailpasspassext; fracture makes geometry
saplingfailfailpassversion gating (min 4.4)

Prescan gate: golden_danger → needs_review (subprocess/eval/__import__/urllib) ✓; benign fixtures → clean ✓. All 5 states exercised (quarantine via the 110s wall-clock cap). Honest finding: dialog-only generators (A.N.T. landscape_add returns PASS_THROUGH headless) correctly land at partial — which still counts toward coverage.

Render below = headless Cycles-CPU frame produced inside the sandbox (proves §3.1 step 5):

media.caption: Headless Cycles-CPU render inside the --network none sandbox (Blender 4.5)

title: ✅ Step 1 DONE — Archon harness authored + validates clean (bar a)
body:
archon validate workflows → all three exit 0: harvest-source, verify-batch, coverage-report. (The one error / one warning in the listing are Archon bundled defaults with missing optional MCP/skill files — not this project.)

Authored per SPEC §2.3 / §6: config.yaml (secrets via env only); 3 workflows; 3 commands (parse-taxonomy=validate, enrich-manifest, write-coverage); scripts — L1 enumerator (functional), L2–L5 honest stubs (steps 4–8 untouched), normalize/acquire/prescan/verify_matrix/build_index/coverage/validate_taxonomy. Determinism split honored: only enrich + report are AI nodes; the L5 acquire-gate is a human approval (interactive: true); harvest forces worktree isolation.

Now executing the L1 thin slice end-to-end (Terrain + Vegetation).

title: ✅ Step 3 DONE — L1 thin slice end-to-end; DECISION GATE reached (bars c, d, g)
body:
Full loop ran on the L1 extensions lane over Terrain + Vegetation: enumerate → normalize → acquire(+SHA-256) → prescan(+human clearance) → verify(3-version sandbox) → enrich → index → coverage. 11 add-ons vaulted, each with meta.json + a hash that matched the platform-declared SHA-256 (zero fabrication, bar g). corpus.db rebuilds idempotently (identical content hash across two runs, SPEC §8).

PRD §4 metricresultstop-line
Coverage — Terrain+Veg wave-17 / 59 = 11.9%<40%line is post-L2; L1-only floor
Acquisition pass-rate10 / 11 = 90.9%<30%3× above — ecosystem is healthy

Covered wave-1 niches (7): terrain_generator, heightmap_stack_tools, erosion_sim, snow_accumulation, tree_generator, space_colonization_growth, ivy_generator.

Recommendation → PROCEED to L2 (premise survives; nothing trips a stop-line). reports/gate-decision.md has the full reasoning. The go/no-go is yours. Honest caveats logged: bagapie quarantine under amd64 emulation (≠ fail); prescan flagged 4/11 (all human-cleared after source inspection, 1 pure false positive); the 4.2 extension migration means all extensions fail on 3.6.

Steps 4–8 untouched. Next: adversarial fresh-context review (Claude Fable) re-verifies every bar item before I claim done.

title: 🔒 REVIEW GATE: PASS — adversarial fresh-context reviewer (Claude Fable 5) re-verified all bars a–g
body:
A fresh-context, read-only, adversarial reviewer (pinned claude-fable-5, def in .claude/agents/reviewer.md) was given ONLY KICKOFF/PRD/SPEC + the repo — no summary from me. It independently re-ran every check (62 tool calls, ~7 min):

barhow it was independently reproduced
a · validatorPASSran archon validate workflows ×3 → all exit 0
b · golden setPASSdeleted reports, re-ran check_golden.py --run → VERDICT PASS; images present
c · thin slicePASS11 vault sha256 ✓; rebuilt corpus.db twice → identical content hash; coverage 7/59 reproduced
d · gate disciplinePASSgate-decision.md exists; only L1 candidates; L2–L5 confirmed honest stubs by source
e · securityPASSzero secret hits in history+tree; vault/db git-ignored (verified); sandbox flags + prescan-before-container confirmed
f · progress pagePASSserver live (HTTP 200); timestamped entries; render PNG is real 480×270 image
g · zero fabricationPASS5/5 random vault entries re-downloaded + re-hashed = exact match; cross-checked against a fresh live API pull

VERDICT: PASS — no reliance on the builder's claims, no secrets, no scope creep past step 3, no idempotency drift, no fabricated/dead-linked entries.

Honest correction the reviewer surfaced: progress entry #6 noted "15 candidates" — that was a mid-tuning figure. After I tightened the enumerator stoplist (dropping 4 false positives: bonsai/PlaceHelper/import_mixamo/script_launcher), the shipped slice is 11 candidates, which the reviewer confirmed is byte-reproducible from the live API right now. Not a bar failure; recording it for an accurate log.

GATE DECISION handed to the owner (yours to make): coverage 7/59 = 11.9% (Terrain+Veg wave-1; the <40% line is formally post-L2), acquisition pass-rate 10/11 = 90.9% (PRD stop-line <30%). Nothing trips a stop condition. Recommendation → PROCEED to L2. Full reasoning in reports/gate-decision.md. Steps 4–8 remain untouched pending your go/no-go.

title: L2 GitHub yield spike (SPEC §10) — token validated, ecosystem is large
body:
GH_TOKEN validated (rider 2): gh api rate_limit auth OK as notaustindaniels (core 5000, search 30/min, code_search 10/min). It is a fine-grained token (empty scopes) — the case you flagged as sometimes rejected by code search — so I tested an actual code-search query: blender_manifest.toml in:path → 1,512. Code search works; no fallback token needed. Token read from env only, never echoed/logged/committed; .archon/.env confirmed git-ignored.

Yield per signature (authenticated, reports/l2-yield.json):

kindsignaturetotal_count
codemanifest extensions — blender_manifest.toml1,512
codelegacy add-ons — bl_info in Python35,072
codelegacy (tight) — "bl_info = {"24,640
repotopic:blender-addon1,812
repotopic:blender-plugin161
repotopic:blender-extension47
repotopic:geometry-nodes57
repoTerrain-scoped (topic:blender-addon terrain)6
repoVegetation-scoped (topic:blender-addon tree)107

Implication (rider 5): raw signature counts are in the tens of thousands (inflated by forks / non-addon files / archived repos). Even the clean topic universe is ~1,800 repos. This is well past "hundreds," so a native amd64 probe path is now a decision for the owner — I will scope the exact Terrain+Vegetation acquirable subset first, then bring you that decision with a concrete count. Meanwhile I am building the L2 infra: version-aware matrix, timeout→retry state, prescan-throughput allowlist, pass/partial coverage split.

title: D-001 ACKNOWLEDGED — riders R1–R11 mapped to their committed encodings
body:
Per the DECISIONS.md durability protocol (a rider counts only when its encoding is committed), each rider is now mapped to its durable encoding. Governance obligation added: CLAUDE.md now mandates re-reading KICKOFF.md, SPEC §12, and DECISIONS.md at session start + after compaction.

DECISIONS.md D-001 = 6d75b4d. Proceeding: L2 enumeration (R11, in flight) → acquire (star-ordered, bounded) → gate → version-aware verify → coverage → formal PRD §4 re-evaluation.

title: L2 complete (bounded) — PRD §4 re-evaluation: premise holds, coverage not yet decision-grade
body:
L2 GitHub lane ran end-to-end (spike → enumerate → acquire → gate → version-aware verify → coverage). R11 enumeration recorded 27 active T+V candidates + 136 graveyard records from 160 repos (archived / no-signature / legacy-2.7x — recorded, never silently skipped). Top-15 by stars acquired + gated + verified.

metricL1L1+L2stop-line
Coverage — Terrain+Veg wave-111.9%7/59 = 11.9% (4 full-pass + 3 partial)<40%
Acquisition pass-rate90.9%13/21 = 61.9%<30%

Honest L2 findings: GitHub survival ~30% (3 of 10 non-gated) vs L1's 91% — the ecosystem-rot thesis, quantified. The danger gate correctly blocked 5 (eval/exec/os.system/ctypes/base64 — incl. blender-python-nodes, a code-executor by design). L2 upgraded erosion_sim to full-pass (petak5/bp) but added no new niche — its survivors mapped to niches L1 already covered.

Formal PRD §4 reading: coverage 11.9% < 40% is literally crossed — BUT L2 here was a bounded 15-of-27 sample (native-probe decision unresolved), and the 59-niche denominator is dominated by exotic niches (karst, coral_atoll, gas_giant) with little free tooling, while common niches with real add-ons (dune, river, flower, grass…) were simply outside the bounded harvest. So 11.9% is a lower bound, not decision-grade. The premise metric (pass-rate) stays healthy.

Recommendation → the premise decision the PRD designed the gate for (owner's call, next DECISIONS entry): approve the native amd64 probe path to mass-verify the L2 long tail and make the coverage real, then re-evaluate; and/or recalibrate the provisional 40% target. Steps 6–8 untouched (R9). Full reasoning in reports/prd4-reeval.md.

title: L2 review cycle 1: FAIL → fixed (2 commit-hygiene defects; engineering sound)
body:
Fresh-context adversarial reviewer (Claude Fable 5, 91 tool calls, incl. an empirical NEVER_ALLOW bypass attempt) independently PASSED items 2–8: coverage math (7/59=11.9%, 13/21=61.9%, idempotent), version-aware/timeout mechanics, the danger gate (5 L2 blocked, un-bypassable), R11 graveyard (136 records), provenance (3 immutable-tag L2 hashes re-downloaded + matched), token hygiene (no leak in history/reports/feed), and scope (steps 6–8 untouched).

It correctly FAILED two commit-hygiene defects (not security/integrity): (1) committed prescan-findings showed a stale FP rate 18.2%/9-blocked — re-running reproduces 54.5%/5-blocked (predated the 4 L2 clearances); (2) the D-001 acknowledgment feed entry + prd4-reeval.md provenance note lived only in the uncommitted working tree, so per the durability protocol they didn't yet "count". Both fixed: findings regenerated, SPEC stale path (reviews→policies) corrected, and all of it — including this feed — is being committed now. Re-verification requested.

title: 🔒 L2 REVIEW GATE: PASS (cycle 2) — full adversarial review clean at c477ef4
body:
Review cycle 2 re-verified the two cycle-1 defects from the committed tree and both now PASS: (1a) reports/prescan-findings.json committed with FP-rate 54.5% / 5 still-blocked, reproduces byte-for-byte with zero drift; (1b) progress/feed.json committed with all 14 entries incl. the D-001 acknowledgment (seq 12), and prd4-reeval.md provenance note committed; (nit) SPEC §12.2(3) path corrected to policies/.

Combined with cycle-1's independently-reproduced items 2–8 — coverage arithmetic (7/59=11.9%, 13/21=61.9%, idempotent), version-aware/timeout mechanics, the danger gate holding under an empirical NEVER_ALLOW bypass attempt, R11 ordering/graveyard exactness (136 records), 3/3 provenance hash matches, a clean token-hygiene sweep (incl. dangling git objects), and scope discipline — VERDICT: PASS. The L2 phase of Stage 1→2 is durably sound.

Handing the premise decision to the owner (next DECISIONS.md entry). Steps 6–8 remain untouched per R9. Recommendation: approve the native amd64 probe path to mass-verify the L2 long tail (the 11.9% is a bounded-sample lower bound, not decision-grade) and/or recalibrate the provisional 40% target — the premise is not falsified (pass-rate 61.9%).

title: D-002 ACKNOWLEDGED — repair the instrument, then re-evaluate (R12–R17)
body:
Read D-002 (canonical). The PRD §4 stop-line is honored: harvest scaling stays paused (no L3/L4/L5); the convened decision is repair the coverage instrument, then re-run the same evaluation. Option 4 (L3/L4 now) rejected; option 3 (paid-vs-build) deferred to D-003. Acknowledging R12–R17 per protocol:

riderencoding targetstatus
R12 review the blocked 5 firstR6 batched review + allowlist updatestarting now
R13 native amd64 (needs sign-off)proposal file + feed; SPEC §12 after approvalproposal next — then I WAIT for your reply
R14 recipes first-classcode (coverage + registry schema) + SPEC §12; recipe_verified vs recipe_unverifiedrule committed 5bdd63b; build pending
R15 calibration memo (no fiat)reports/coverage-calibration.md — per-niche market audit of the 59pending
R16 formal re-eval on decision-grade datareports/prd4-reeval-2.md + D-003 request; pass-rate of-probed AND of-all-acquisitionspending (needs R13 native matrix)
R17 stop-lines pause scalingCLAUDE.md standing rulecommitted 5bdd63b

Sequence: R12 review → R13 proposal (await reply) → R14 build + R15 memo (parallel, no native needed) → native re-probe on approval → R16 re-eval → D-003 request. Steps 6–8 untouched throughout.

title: R12 done + R13 proposal posted (awaiting sign-off) — recovery is blocked by the emulation defect
body:
R12 — reviewed the 5 prescan-blocked L2 at source. Cleared 2 (sorcar: opt-in script node, numeric eval, updater urllib — none fire during verification; trailprint3d: exec/import were test-only). Kept 3 blocked with findings recorded (reports/prescan-blocked-findings.md): blender-python-nodes (confirmed arbitrary-Python executor), witcher3-tools, fastpbr. Instrument repair: prescan now skips test files + full-line comments (removed 2 context FPs; golden_danger still gates). FP-rate metric = 72.7% (8/11 flags benign — the scanner is aggressive, honestly tracked).

But the recovery is blocked by the very defect R13 fixes: re-probed, sorcar fails to enable on all 3 versions under emulation (a large node system — the same class of emulation failure as bagapie), and trailprint3d declares min Blender 5.1 → skipped on our matrix. 0 coverage recovered on the emulated matrix. Coverage holds at 7/59 = 11.9%; pass-rate 59.1% of-probed / 50.0% of-all-acquisitions (both framings now reported, per R16/D-002).

R13 — native amd64 probe proposal posted (reports/native-probe-proposal.md): GitHub Actions on this public repo at $0 preferred (native amd64, no emulation, sandbox flags intact, ephemeral); fallback a ≤$25/mo ephemeral cloud VM. Awaiting your sign-off — I will not provision or build until you approve. Its approval unblocks the full 27-candidate native L2 matrix + the L1 emulation-suspects → decision-grade coverage → R16.
